compiled_state.title: release.sh: tag created before version bump commit -> v1.13.0 tag points to wrong commit
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
During release 1.13.0, release.sh created the git tag v1.13.0 pointing to the pre-version-bump commit (aa92a10c, plugin.json=1.12.56), then committed the version bump separately as 5e1e36a4. The result: v1.13.0 tag and plugin.json version were misaligned until manually corrected via force-push.

## Expected Behavior
The tag v1.13.0 should point to a commit where plugin.json already declares version 1.13.0.

## Actual Behavior
Tag was created on the pre-bump commit. Downstream consumers installing via the tag would get plugin.json=1.12.56, not 1.13.0.

## Root Cause
release.sh creates the tag and then edits/commits the version bump as a separate step. The tag should either be created after the version bump commit, or the version bump should be included in the tag creation commit.

## Fix Direction
Reorder release.sh: bump version in plugin.json FIRST, commit, THEN create and push the tag.

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence) — no design doc or ADR justifies tag pointing to pre-bump commit. Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC tier). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=0, intermittent=0
comments.author: Test
